title: Library Catalog Schema
description: JSON Schema for library.yaml — the catalog file for the cognovis-library private skill distribution system.

default_dirs.description: Default installation directories per primitive type and harness.

skills.description: Directory mappings for skills across harnesses.

agents.description: Directory mappings for agents across harnesses.

prompts.description: Directory mappings for prompts/commands across harnesses.

scripts.description: Directory mappings for first-class Python script primitives.

library.description: Canonical primitive catalog. All primitive entry arrays live here; legacy root primitive arrays are accepted only as migration aliases.

standards.description: Catalog of standard entries. Standards are policy/convention documents that apply across harnesses (e.g. coding rules, control-area baselines). Cross-harness storage convention: .agents/standards/<name>.md (project) and ~/.agents/standards/<name>.md (global).

scripts.description: Catalog of first-class Python script primitives. Scripts are deterministic helpers consumed by skills, agents, hooks, commands, standards, and pack exporters.

mcp_servers.description: Catalog of canonical MCP server model definitions.

agent_bases.description: Catalog of Layer 1 agent base prompt entries.

sources.description: Canonical source registries used to resolve catalog entries. `sources.catalogs` owns first-party source repositories; `sources.marketplaces` owns third-party source providers.

guardrails.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for library.guardrails. New catalog files should use library.guardrails.

tag_vocabulary.description: Catalog-owned tag definitions used for search and filtering across primitive entries.

standards.description: Storage convention and loader-mechanism reference (stub — populated by CL-v56).

mcp_servers.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for library.mcp_servers. New catalog files should use library.mcp_servers.

marketplaces.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for sources.marketplaces. New catalog files should use sources.marketplaces.

catalog.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for sources.catalogs. New catalog files should use sources.catalogs.

project_tooling.description: Fleet-wide per-project file/hook distribution entries. Each entry declares a target that should be synced into every matching project at SessionStart. Supports file copy, git hook installation, JSON field enforcement, and more.

model_standards.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for library.model_standards. New catalog files should use library.model_standards.

tag_definition.description: A documented catalog tag that may appear in entry tags arrays.

catalog_entry.description: A first-party content repo (personal or team) registered as a canonical source.

name.description: Unique identifier for this catalog repo (kebab-case recommended).

description.description: Human-readable description of the repo's purpose.

owner.description: GitHub user or org that owns this repo.

audience.description: Intended audience: personal, team, or public.

local_path.description: Optional filesystem checkout path for this source provider. Use null for remote-only sources.

writable.description: True when promotion tools may create or update primitives in this source provider.

content_types.description: Types of content that live in this repo.

scope.description: Routing scope metadata used by catalog match.

topics.description: Topic tags this source provider accepts.

excludes.description: Topic tags this source provider explicitly does not accept.

skeleton.description: Directory skeleton per harness — documents what directories exist in this repo.

notes.description: Free-form notes about this catalog entry.

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.

dir_list.description: A list of named directory mappings (key: path pairs).

additionalProperties.description: Path to the directory (absolute, relative, or ~ prefix).

source_ref.description: Source reference for a catalog entry — local path or GitHub URL.

typed_dependency.description: A typed dependency reference using 'type:name' format. Types per ADR-0004 Decision 2 plus later primitive additions.

script_asset.description: A deterministic Python script bundled with or referenced by a primitive.

path.description: Path to the Python script relative to the primitive root.

role.description: How the script is used by the owning primitive.

entrypoint.description: True when the script is directly callable by humans, pack commands, doctor checks, or formulas.

language.description: Script implementation language. Cognovis Library scripts are Python-only.

gascity_export.description: Library-owned Gas City PackV2 export metadata. This is a projection hint for pack generation, not Gas City runtime config.

exportable.description: Whether this primitive may be exported into a Gas City pack.

target.description: Legacy Gas City pack surface this primitive projects to. Prefer projections[].target for new metadata.

pack.description: Legacy preferred pack name for generated exports, e.g. cognovis-base. Prefer projections[].pack for new metadata.

scope.description: Legacy runtime scope for the exported behavior. Prefer projections[].scope for new metadata.

projections.description: Gas City PackV2 projection targets. Use this shape when target, scope, or target_path varies per pack.

session_class.description: Whether this primitive implies a Gas City transient or persistent session class.

provider_neutral.description: True when core content avoids Claude/Codex/provider-specific assumptions.

requires.description: Runtime requirements for the exported pack surface.

gascity_target_surface.description: Gas City pack surface a Library primitive projects to.

gascity_scope.description: Where exported Gas City behavior is expected to operate.

gascity_session_class.description: Whether a Gas City projection implies a transient or persistent session class.

gascity_requires.description: Runtime requirements for a projected pack surface.

gascity_projection.description: One Gas City PackV2 projection target for a Library primitive.

target.description: Gas City pack surface this primitive projects to.

pack.description: Target PackV2 pack name, e.g. cognovis-base.

scope.description: Where the exported behavior is expected to operate.

session_class.description: Whether this projection implies a Gas City transient or persistent session class.

provider_neutral.description: True when core content avoids Claude/Codex/provider-specific assumptions.

requires.description: Runtime requirements for this projected pack surface.

product_counterpart.description: Reference to paired product-plane work. The referenced product artifact is not a Library catalog primitive.

repo.description: Product repository or product identifier, e.g. mira or polaris.

path.description: Optional path to the paired product-plane artifact.

notes.description: Free-form notes about the product-plane counterpart.

entry_metadata.description: Open metadata bag. Library-owned metadata lives under metadata.library.*; agentskills-compatible string fields may coexist at the top level.

plane.description: Library catalog plane. Catalog entries are dev-plane primitives; product-plane artifacts are referenced via product_counterpart.

base_entry.description: Common fields shared by all catalog entry types. Either `source` or `from_marketplace + repo + path` is required.

name.description: Unique identifier for this catalog entry.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this entry provides.

source.description: Location of the source file (local path or GitHub URL).

requires.description: Typed dependencies that must be installed before this entry.

scripts.description: Python scripts bundled with this primitive and visible to validators/exporters.

version.description: Optional semantic version or tag of the entry.

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.

marketplace_ref.description: Optional marketplace reference fields for catalog entries sourced from a marketplace.

from_marketplace.description: Short name of the marketplace this entry is sourced from (must match a name in the marketplaces list).

path.description: Path within the repository to the skill/agent/prompt directory.

marketplace_entry.description: A marketplace source reference for external skill/agent/prompt libraries.

source.description: Base GitHub URL or organization URL for this marketplace.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this marketplace provides.

auth.description: Authentication method required to access this marketplace.

local_path.description: Optional filesystem checkout path for this marketplace. Use null for remote-only sources.

writable.description: True when promotion tools may create or update primitives in this marketplace.

content_types.description: Types of content this marketplace provides.

scope.description: Routing scope metadata used by catalog match.

excludes.description: Topic tags this marketplace explicitly does not accept.

skill_entry.description: A skill catalog entry with install path information.

install_path.description: Override the default install directory for this skill.

harness.description: Target harness for this skill (claude, codex, or both).

globs.description: File glob patterns (agentskills.io standard). When any glob matches a file in context, this skill is suggested.

always_apply.description: When true, always load this skill into context regardless of file matches (agentskills.io standard). Use sparingly.

compatibility.description: Minimum harness version or capability string per agentskills.io standard (e.g. 'claude_code>=4.0').

agent_entry.description: An agent catalog entry with format-translation hints.

install_path.description: Override the default install directory for this agent.

format.description: Agent file format (for translation hints between harnesses).

sources.description: Per-harness source file paths. Keys are harness names (claude, codex). Values are relative paths in the source repo. Use instead of the singular source: field when the agent ships multiple harness-specific files. Backward compat: singular source: still works for Claude-only agents.

claude.description: Path or URL to the Claude-format agent file (.md with YAML frontmatter).

codex.description: Path or URL to the Codex-format agent file (.toml with developer_instructions).

install_path.description: Override the default install directory for this prompt.

standard_entry.description: A standard catalog entry — a policy/convention document or bundle of related convention docs. Cross-harness; install path defaults to .agents/standards/<name>/ (bundle = directory) or .agents/standards/<name>.md (single file). Source may be a GitHub blob URL (single file) or tree URL (directory bundle).

install_path.description: Override the default install directory for this standard.

triggers.description: Legacy compatibility metadata for older catalog entries. Standards are no longer loaded by automatic project-context matching; installation plus explicit consumer references such as requires_standards are the delivery contract. Prefer globs or always_apply only when exporting to agentskills-compatible metadata.

globs.description: File glob patterns (agentskills.io standard). When any glob matches a file in context, this standard is suggested.

always_apply.description: When true, always load this standard into context regardless of file matches (agentskills.io standard). Use sparingly.

script_entry.description: A first-class Python script primitive. Scripts are deterministic helpers, not model context, and may be exported to Gas City assets/scripts, commands, doctor checks, or formula steps.

install_path.description: Override the default install directory for this script.

language.description: Script implementation language. Cognovis Library scripts are Python-only.

entrypoint.description: Path to the primary Python entrypoint relative to the source directory, or omitted when source points directly at the .py file.

gascity_targets.description: Allowed Gas City target surfaces for this script.

mcp_server_entry.description: Canonical definition of one MCP server. Describes what the server does and how it should be installed per harness — translation logic is a follow-up bead.

name.description: Unique identifier for this MCP server (kebab-case recommended).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this MCP server provides.

source.description: Optional upstream source URL (GitHub browser/raw URL or absolute local path) pointing to a canonical manifest file in the MCP server's source repo. When present, `library status`/`library sync` compares the installed lockfile SHA against this file's upstream commit. Omit for built-in or unversioned MCP entries.

coding_strategy.description: How coding harnesses (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode) should consume this server: cli = install the CLI wrapper package, mcp = add to harness MCP config.

mobile_strategy.description: How mobile/web harnesses (claude.ai, claude.ios) consume this server.

capabilities.description: Runtime characteristics of this MCP server.

stateless.description: True if the server holds no persistent state between calls.

streaming.description: True if the server supports streaming responses.

auth.description: Authentication mechanism required by this server.

install.description: Per-harness install metadata. Does NOT include translator code — that is a follow-up bead.

cli.description: Install metadata when coding_strategy is cli.

package.description: Package name to install (e.g. npm package or PyPI name).

mcp.description: Per-harness MCP config hints when coding_strategy or mobile_strategy is mcp. Keys are harness names (claude_code, codex, opencode, claude_ai, claude_ios). Values are open objects — the translator bead will define stricter schemas.

config_path.description: Path to the harness config file that receives the snippet.

snippet.description: Canonical MCP server descriptor. Structure is harness-specific — translator bead enforces exact shape.

config_path.description: Path to the harness config file that receives the snippet.

snippet.description: Canonical MCP server descriptor. Structure is harness-specific — translator bead enforces exact shape.

config_path.description: Path to the harness config file that receives the snippet.

snippet.description: Canonical MCP server descriptor. Structure is harness-specific — translator bead enforces exact shape.

claude_ai.description: Config hints for claude.ai (web — manual install via URL).

install_url.description: URL the user opens to add this server to claude.ai.

claude_ios.description: Config hints for Claude iOS (manual install via URL).

install_url.description: URL the user opens to add this server to Claude iOS.

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.

guardrail_entry.description: A guardrail catalog entry — a deterministic enforcement primitive that compiles to per-harness native format. Guardrails run outside the LLM loop and cannot be bypassed by the model.

name.description: Unique identifier for this guardrail (kebab-case recommended).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this guardrail enforces.

kind.description: Installation shape per ADR-0004 Phase 2. 'single-hook' (default) is one script bound to one event/harness (sources field used). 'hooks-manifest' is a multi-event manifest file (source field used, or sources.<harness> for per-harness manifests) — install logic merges all declared hooks into harness config.

source.description: URL of the manifest file (required when kind=hooks-manifest unless sources.<harness> is provided). For single-hook entries use the 'sources' map instead. Typically a hooks.json from a marketplace repo.

requires.description: Transitive dependencies on other library primitives (skill:, agent:, prompt:, hook:, mcp:). Authoritative source is the artefact's frontmatter; this mirror is for offline lookup.

harness.description: Which harnesses this guardrail targets (claude, codex, both, all).

purpose.description: Lifecycle class of this guardrail (single-hook entries). Determines which harnesses can support it natively vs. via workaround.

capability.description: Per-harness capability declarations. Only harnesses listed here are supported. The installer uses this to emit mismatch warnings when a target harness lacks the required event.

claude_code.description: Capability declaration for Claude Code (hooks via settings.json). Supports 13+ events.

codex_cli.description: Capability declaration for Codex CLI hooks.json.

codex_cloud.description: Capability declaration for Codex cloud (sandbox_mode + approval_policy in config.toml). Pre-tool only.

pi.description: Capability declaration for Pi harness (TypeScript extensions in .pi/extensions/*.ts).

opencode.description: Capability declaration for OpenCode (permission rules in opencode.json). Pre-tool gates.

sources.description: Per-harness sources. For single-hook entries these are source file paths relative to repo root. For hooks-manifest entries these may be manifest URLs per harness.

claude_code.description: Path to the Claude Code hook script (bash/python), or manifest URL for kind=hooks-manifest.

codex_cli.description: Path to the Codex CLI hook script, or manifest URL for kind=hooks-manifest.

codex_cloud.description: Path to the Codex cloud config fragment (TOML).

pi.description: Path to the Pi TypeScript extension file (.ts).

opencode.description: Path to the OpenCode permission rule fragment (JSON).

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.

tooling_condition.description: A condition that must be true for this target to be applied. Exactly one key per condition object.

dir_exists.description: Path (relative to project root) that must be a directory

file_exists.description: Path (relative to project root) that must be a file

command_available.description: Command name that must be on PATH (via which)

env_set.description: Environment variable name that must be set

project_tooling_entry.description: A project tooling distribution entry — declares a target (file, git hook, JSON field block, etc.) that should be synced into matching projects at SessionStart.

name.description: Unique identifier for this tooling entry (kebab-case recommended).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this entry does and why it exists.

target_kind.description: Type of target this entry manages. Determines which sync logic is applied.

target_path.description: Path to the target location, relative to the project root.

source.description: Source path relative to the library root. Required for file, file_section, git_hook, and gitignore_patch target_kinds.

conditions.description: List of conditions that must all be true before this entry is applied. Supported keys: dir_exists, file_exists, command_available, env_set.

profiles.description: Optional project profiles this tooling entry applies to. Omit to apply to every profile.

sync_strategy.description: How to apply the target when syncing. overwrite_if_source_newer: copy only when content differs. overwrite_always: always overwrite. append_if_missing: append content if not already present. replace_section: replace a marked section between begin/end markers. repair_fields: for json_field_enforce, apply ensure/remove field operations.

conflict_policy.description: What to do when the target has been locally modified. canonical_wins: always overwrite with canonical source. user_wins: skip if target exists and has local changes. warn_only: log a warning but do not overwrite.

consumed_by.description: Optional hint about which tool and command reads this target after it is synced.

tool.description: Name of the tool that reads this target (e.g. bd, git, node).

command.description: Specific command invocation that reads this target (e.g. bd prime).

fields.description: For json_field_enforce target_kind: declares fields to ensure/set and field names to remove. For gitignore_patch: declares exact lines to ensure or remove.

ensure.description: Key/value pairs that must be present (and equal) in the target JSON file.

remove.description: Field names that must be absent from the target JSON file.

ensure_lines.description: Exact lines that must be present in a text target such as .gitignore.

remove_lines.description: Exact lines that must be absent from a text target such as .gitignore.

hook_name.description: For git_hook target_kind: the git hook name (e.g. post-commit, pre-commit, pre-push).

section_markers.description: For file_section and gitignore_patch target_kinds: begin/end comment markers that delimit the managed section.

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.

model_standard_entry.description: A Layer 3 model-standard catalog entry. Describes behavioral guidelines for a specific model. Installed to .agents/model-standards/<name>.md (project) or ~/.agents/model-standards/<name>.md (global).

name.description: Unique identifier for this model-standard (kebab-case, matches the filename without .md extension).

description.description: Human-readable description of what model this standard applies to and what behavior it enforces.

source.description: GitHub URL or local path to the model-standard file.

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.

agent_base_entry.description: A Layer 1 agent base prompt catalog entry. Describes the base behavioral document prepended to every composed agent. Installed to .agents/agent-bases/<name>.md (project) or ~/.agents/agent-bases/<name>.md (global).

name.description: Unique identifier for this agent base prompt (kebab-case, matches the filename without .md extension).

description.description: Human-readable description of the agent base prompt's purpose and behavioral scope.

source.description: GitHub URL or local path to the agent base prompt file.

requires.description: Transitive dependencies on other library primitives.

tags.description: Optional tags for search and filtering.
